strengths: Maintained steady traction throughout the cutting sequence
Completed the full circle path without stopping or hesitation
Demonstrated good instrument coordination during the cutting motion
weaknesses: Cut path deviated visibly from the marked circle boundaries
Completion time of 110 seconds exceeds intermediate target range
Inconsistent precision in following the circular guideline

drills.name: Circle tracking quarter-cuts
drills.description: Cut the circle in four controlled quadrants while maintaining traction, pausing between sections to verify alignment with the marked boundary.
drills.targets_weakness: Cut path deviated visibly from the marked circle boundaries

drills.name: Traction-angle practice
drills.description: 10 repetitions of repositioning the Maryland to improve scissor entry angle and maintain optimal cutting geometry throughout the circle.
drills.targets_weakness: Inconsistent precision in following the circular guideline

drills.name: Economy-of-motion ladder
drills.description: Perform the circle cutting task slowly with minimal wasted regrasping, focusing on smooth continuous motion to improve time efficiency.
drills.targets_weakness: Completion time of 110 seconds exceeds intermediate target range

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your consistent cutting motion into precise boundary following while reducing completion time to under 90 seconds.
